_doc: Machine-readable CLI contract for tt-download. Agents can parse this to construct invocations or validate arguments without re-reading SKILL.md.
summary: Resolve the signed video URL behind an Oceanengine 素材中心 video_player page; optionally download.

arguments.name: url
arguments.description: Full video_player URL, or '@<path>' to read from a file.

options.description: Save the video stream to this path. Omit to only print the resolved URL.
Also print the intermediate cc.oceanengine.com URL to stderr.

stdout: The resolved https://video-cn.oceanengine.com/... URL (one line). Empty on failure.
stderr: Progress and error logging. Includes 'intermediate: <cc-url>' when --show-intermediate is set, and '✅ 已下载 <size> → <path>' after a successful download.

exit_codes.0: Success — URL resolved (and downloaded if -o given).
exit_codes.1: Failure during extraction or download (token expired, browser missing, network error, HTTP 4xx/5xx from CDN).
exit_codes.2: Invalid CLI usage (missing or empty URL).

examples.intent: Print only the resolved video URL
Also echo the intermediate cc URL

timeouts.render: Chrome --dump-dom runs with --virtual-time-budget=30000; subprocess timeout is 60s.
timeouts.resolve: cc URL HTTP request times out at 30s.
timeouts.download: urllib download times out at 60s on initial connect; reads are blocking per chunk.

output_naming._doc: Suggested default filenames when the user does not specify -o.
rules: If the URL token decodes to a knowable material id, use 'material-<id>.mp4'.
Otherwise use 'oceanengine-<YYYYmmdd-HHMMSS>.mp4' stamped at extraction time.
Always echo the chosen path to stderr so the agent can confirm.
